= Archet Asturias =

{{CharacterInfobox
| name        = Archet Asturias
| portrait    = https://foundry.atkennedy.com/assets/Images/Tokens/Ghoran%20token.png
| level       = 5
| size        = Medium
| hp          = 68/68
| ac          = 22
| perception  = +10
| speed       = 25 ft.
| fortitude   = +9
| reflex      = +13
| will        = +10
| attributes  = '''STR''' +0 | '''DEX''' +4 | '''CON''' +2 | '''INT''' +2 | '''WIS''' +1 | '''CHA''' +3
| key_ability  = STR
| age          = 18
| gender       = she/her
| hero_points  = 0
| languages   = dwarven, shadowtongue
| wealth      = 10 GP
}}

== Details ==
* '''Ancestry:''' Ghoran
* '''Heritage:''' Strong Oak
* '''Background:''' Framed in Ferrous Quarter
* '''Class:''' <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/classes/swashbuckler.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Swashbuckler" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> Swashbuckler
* '''Key Ability:''' STR
* '''Languages:''' dwarven, shadowtongue

== Identity ==
* '''Age:''' 18
* '''Nationality:''' Garundian

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>⚔ Defense & Saves</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
* '''HP:''' 68/68
* '''AC:''' 22
* '''Perception:''' +10 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Speed:''' 25 ft. (land)
* '''Fortitude:''' +9 ({{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}})
* '''Reflex:''' +13 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
* '''Will:''' +10 ({{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}})
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>📊 Ability Scores</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; width:100%;"
|-
! STR !! DEX !! CON !! INT !! WIS !! CHA
|-
| +0 || +4 || +2 || +2 || +1 || +3
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎓 Skills</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="width:100%;"
! Skill !! Modifier !! Proficiency
|-
| Acrobatics || +13 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Arcana || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Athletics || +7 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Crafting || +9 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Deception || +10 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Diplomacy || +10 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Engineering Lore || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Games Lore || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Intimidation || +12 || {{color|#4682b4|'''Expert'''}}
|-
| Medicine || +1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Nature || +1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Occultism || +2 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Performance || +3 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Religion || +8 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Society || +9 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Stealth || +11 || {{color|#2e8b57|'''Trained'''}}
|-
| Survival || +1 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|-
| Thievery || +4 || {{color|#708090|'''Untrained'''}}
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>💰 Wealth & Carry</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
{| class="wikitable"
! Coin !! Total Value !! Bulk !! Invested
|-
| 10 GP || 10 gp || 14.1 / 5 enc / 10 max || 0/10
|}
</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>✨ Feats & Features</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Heritage=====
; '''Strong Oak'''
: Most ghorans have bodies of pliable vines and tough stems, but you're covered in sturdy, rough bark and your legs are stable as roots. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus on your Fortitude or Reflex DC against attempts to Grapple or Trip you. This bonus also applies to saving throws against effects that would Grab you, Restrain you, or knock you Prone. You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus on Acrobatics checks to Balance.

=====Background Features=====
; '''Framed in Ferrous Quarter'''
: Everyone knows that Ferrous Quarter isn't exactly the nicest part of Alkenstar. Hard-working laborers, gunsmiths suffering from black lung, and red-eyed tinkerers alike can toil their entire lives on these grimy streets and still wind up dead in an alley with nothing to show for it. You were once one of these humble artisans, until one day, out of nowhere, things broke bad.

Maybe you hung out with the wrong crowd. Maybe it was just bad luck. Whatever the case, you were framed for a crime you didn't commit-larceny, smuggling, possibly even murder. You didn't stick around long enough to find out what, exactly, the shieldmarshals banging on your door were charging you with. Now, you're on the lam, uncertain where you'll be able to get a good night's sleep, let alone your next meal.

At least one thing's for sure, though: before you turned heel and ran into the streets, you saw a telltale signature on the arrest warrant. It was the scrawl of a corrupt shieldmarshal who's framed countless other hardworking folks in your neighborhood on the basis of cooked-up evidence. Yes, you're sure of it-the crooked guard who took you down is none other than Deputy Anjelique Loveless, and you're determined to return the favor.

Choose two attribute boosts. One must be to '''Dexterity''' or '''Constitution''', and one is a free attribute boost.

You're trained in the Crafting skill and the Engineering Lore skill. You gain the Specialty Crafting skill feat.

=====Feats=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Additional Lore" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Additional Lore''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: Your knowledge has expanded to encompass a new field. Choose a Lore skill subcategory. You become trained in it. At 3rd, 7th, and 15th levels, you gain an additional skill increase you can apply only to the chosen Lore subcategory.

'''Special''' You can select this feat more than once. Each time you must select a new subcategory of Lore and you gain the additional skill increases to that subcategory for the listed levels.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Ancient Memories" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Ancient Memories''' <small>(ghoran)</small>
: In the long years since Ghorus's day, you've done pretty much everything at one time or another. During your daily preparations, you can explore your memories of your past lives to become trained in one skill of your choice. This proficiency lasts until you prepare again. Since this proficiency is temporary, you can't use it as a prerequisite for a skill increase or a permanent character option like a feat.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Antagonize" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Antagonize''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: Your taunts and threats earn your foes' ire. When you successfully Demoralize a creature, its Frightened condition can't decrease to less than 1 at the end of its turn until it either uses a hostile action against you or can no longer observe or sense you for at least 1 round.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/braggart-style.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Braggart" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Braggart''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: You boast, taunt, and psychologically needle your foes. You are trained in Intimidation. When you Demoralize, the action gains the bravado trait.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/swords/scimitar-broad.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Confident Finisher" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Confident Finisher''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: You gain an elegant finishing attack you can make when you have panache. The finisher trait is described below. You gain the Confident Finisher action.

'''Finisher''' Finishers are spectacular finishing moves that use your panache. Finishers can be used only with weapons that deal additional damage with precise strike (agile or finesse melee weapons or unarmed attacks, for most swashbucklers). You can use a finisher only if you have panache, and you lose your panache immediately after performing a finisher. Once you use a finisher, you can't use actions that have the attack trait for the rest of your turn.

Some finisher actions also grant an effect on a failure. Effects added on a failure don't apply on a critical failure. If your finisher action succeeds, you can still choose to apply the failure effect instead. For example, you might do this when an attack deals no damage due to resistance.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Flying Blade" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Flying Blade''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: You apply your flashy techniques to thrown weapons as easily as melee attacks. You apply your Precise Strike damage on ranged Strikes you make with a thrown weapon within that weapon's first range increment. The thrown weapon must be an agile or finesse weapon. This also allows you to make a thrown weapon ranged Strike for Confident Finisher and any other finisher that includes a Strike that can benefit from your precise strike.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/life/cross-explosion-burst-green.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Fortitude Expertise" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Fortitude Expertise''' <small>(animist, bard, druid, investigator, swashbuckler)</small>
: '''Animist''' Spiritual energy reinforces your body, making you more resilient. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to expert.

'''Bard, Investigator, Swashbuckler''' Your physique has grown hardy in your adventures. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to expert.

'''Druid''' Adventures have made your physique more hardy. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to expert.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Intimidating Glare" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Intimidating Glare''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: You can Demoralize with a mere glare. When you do, Demoralize loses the auditory trait and gains the visual trait, and you don't take a penalty if the creature doesn't understand your language.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/flags/banner-symbol-axe-red.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Opportune Riposte" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Opportune Riposte''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: You turn the tables on foes who fumble, capitalizing immediately on their mistake. You gain the Opportune Riposte reaction.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/commodities/treasure/crown-gold-laurel-wreath.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Panache" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Panache''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: You care as much about the way you accomplish something as whether you actually accomplish it in the first place. When you perform an action with particular style, you can leverage this moment of verve to perform spectacular, deadly maneuvers. This heightened state of flair is called panache.

Effect: Panache

You gain panache by performing actions that have the bravado trait. Tumble Through and additional actions determined by your swashbuckler's style gain the bravado trait when you use them. The GM might determine that a check to perform a particularly daring action, such as swinging on a chandelier or sliding down a drapery, can gain the bravado trait. These checks typically involve at least a single action and a non-trivial DC. Normally, you gain and use panache only in combat encounters; when an encounter ends, you lose panache.

Powerful finisher actions, including Confident Finisher, can be used only while you have panache and cause you to lose your panache.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/nature/root-vine-beanstolk-green.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Photosynthesis" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Photosynthesis'''
: You gain nourishment from photosynthesis. You typically don't need to pay for food. If you go without sunlight for 1 week, you begin to starve. You can derive nourishment from specially formulated bottles of alchemical sunlight instead of natural sunlight, but these bottles cost 10 times as much as standard rations (40 sp).
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/precise-strike.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Precise Strike" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Precise Strike''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: When you make a Strike with an agile or finesse melee weapon or agile or finesse unarmed attack, you deal 2 additional precision damage. If the Strike is part of a finisher, the additional damage is 2d6 precision damage instead.

As your swashbuckler level increases, so does your additional damage for precise strike. At 5th, 9th, 13th, and 17th level, increase the amount of additional damage on a Strike by 1 and the additional damage on a finisher by 1d6.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Reincarnated Ridiculer" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Reincarnated Ridiculer''' <small>(reincarnated)</small>
: You've practiced the art of taunting an enemy, ensuring they know death will only make you stronger. When you Demoralize an opponent, the target doesn't become temporarily immune to your attempts to Demoralize it unless you critically fail the check. If the target critically hit you with a Strike on its previous turn, the DC for this check is reduced by 4.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Specialty Crafting (Woodworking)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Specialty Crafting (Woodworking)''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: Your training focused on Crafting one particular kind of item. Select one of the specialties listed below; you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Crafting checks to Craft items of that type. If you are a master in Crafting, this bonus increases to +2. If it's unclear whether the specialty applies, the GM decides. Some specialties might apply only partially. For example, if you were making a morningstar and had specialty in woodworking, the GM might give you half your bonus because the item requires both blacksmithing and woodworking.

Specialty
Applicable Items

Alchemy*
Alchemical items such as elixirs

Artistry
Fine art, including jewelry

Blacksmithing
Durable metal goods, including metal armor

Bookmaking
Books and paper

Glassmaking
Glass, including glassware and windows

Leatherworking
Leather goods, including leather armor

Pottery
Ceramic goods

Shipbuilding
Ships and boats

Stonemasonry
Stone goods and structures

Tailoring
Clothing

Weaving
Textiles, baskets, and rugs

Woodworking
Wooden goods and structures

* You must have the Alchemical Crafting skill feat to Craft alchemical items.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/melee/maneuver-greatsword-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Stylish Combatant" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Stylish Combatant'''
: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks with the bravado trait while in a combat encounter. While you have panache, you gain a +5-foot status bonus to your Speeds.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/features/classes/stylish-tricks.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Stylish Tricks" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Stylish Tricks''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: At 3rd level, 7th level, and 15th level, you gain an additional skill increase you can apply only to Acrobatics or the skill from your swashbuckler's style. You also gain an additional skill feat at these levels. This feat must be for Acrobatics or the trained skill from your swashbuckler's style.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/magic/symbols/question-stone-yellow.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Swashbuckler's Style" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Swashbuckler's Style''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: Your own distinctive style lets you gracefully handle any situation. Choose a swashbuckler's style. This style determines the additional actions you use to gain panache and makes you trained in the skill related to that action.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Tumbling Teamwork" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Tumbling Teamwork''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: Your tumbling distracts a foe enough to create an advantage for one of your allies. When you successfully Tumble Through an enemy's space, an ally who is adjacent to that enemy can Step as a reaction, but they must remain adjacent to that enemy.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Vicious Critique" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Vicious Critique''' <small>(general, skill)</small>
: '''Trigger''' You are about to attempt to Coerce a merchant or crafter.

You have an eye for detail and a mouth for pointing out flaws, which makes it all the easier to convince the merchants and crafters of King's Heart to help you. You quickly analyze the triggering creature's wares in hopes of finding something useful in your coercion and attempt a Crafting check.

'''Critical Success''' You have plenty to work with and can leave your target shocked with your analysis. If you a roll a success on your Intimidation check, you get a critical success instead. If you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

'''Success''' As critical success, but you only gain the benefits when rolling a success on your Intimidation check.

'''Failure''' You misjudge the wares, which makes you come across as unknowledgeable and less intimidating. You take a –1 circumstance penalty to your Intimidation check to Coerce.

'''Critical Failure''' As failure, but the penalty is –2.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/feet/boots-leather-grey-gold.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Vivacious Speed" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Vivacious Speed''' <small>(swashbuckler)</small>
: When you've made an impression, you move even faster than normal, darting about the battlefield with incredible speed. Increase the status bonus to your Speeds from stylish combatant to a +10-foot status bonus; this bonus increases by 5 feet at 7th, 11th, 15th, and 19th levels. When you don't have panache, you still get half this status bonus to your Speed, rounded down to the nearest 5-foot increment.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/skills/melee/hand-grip-sword-red.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Weapon Expertise" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Weapon Expertise''' <small>(champion, druid, exemplar, investigator, kineticist, magus, oracle, psychic, sorcerer, swashbuckler, witch, wizard)</small>
: '''Champion, Exemplar, Investigator, Magus''' You've dedicated yourself to learning the intricacies of your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks increase to expert.

'''Druid, Kineticist''' You've improved your combat skill. Your proficiency rank for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increases to expert.

'''Oracle''' You've dedicated yourself to learning the intricacies of your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increase to expert.

'''Psychic''' Training and magic improved your weapon technique. Your proficiency rank for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increases to expert.

'''Sorcerer''' You've improved your combat skill. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increase to expert.

'''Swashbuckler''' You've dedicated yourself to learning the intricacies of your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons, martial weapons, and unarmed attacks increase to expert. You gain access to the critical specialization effects of all weapons for which you have expert proficiency.

'''Witch, Wizard''' Through sheer experience, you've improved your technique with your weapons. Your proficiency ranks for simple weapons and unarmed attacks increase to expert.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/books/book-red-exclamation.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="You're Next" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''You're Next''' <small>(emotion, fear, mental, rogue, swashbuckler)</small>
: '''Trigger''' You reduce an enemy to 0 Hit Points.

'''Rogue''' After downing a foe, you menace another. Attempt to Demoralize a creature within 60 feet, with a +2 circumstance bonus. If you have legendary proficiency in Intimidation, you can use this as a free action with the same trigger.

'''Swashbuckler''' After downing a foe, you promise another that you're coming after them next. Attempt an Intimidation check with a +2 circumstance bonus to Demoralize a single creature that you can see and that can see you. If you're legendary in Intimidation, you can use this as a free action with the same trigger.

=====Actions=====
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/OneAction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Confident Finisher" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Confident Finisher''' <small>(finisher)</small>
: You make an incredibly graceful attack, piercing your foe's defenses. Make a Strike with the following failure effect.

'''Failure''' You deal half your Precise Strike damage to the target. This damage type is that of the weapon or unarmed attack you used for the Strike.
; <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/actions/Reaction.webp" width="20" height="20" alt="Opportune Riposte" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> '''Opportune Riposte''' <small>(bravado, swashbuckler)</small>
: '''Trigger''' A foe within your reach critically fails a Strike against you

You take advantage of an opening from your enemy's fumbled attack. You either make a melee Strike against the triggering foe or attempt to Disarm it of the weapon it used for the Strike.

</div>
</div>

<div class="toccolours mw-collapsible mw-collapsed">
<b>🎒 Inventory</b>
<div class="mw-collapsible-content">
=====Weapons=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/weapons/staves/staff-forked-wood.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Filcher's Fork" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Filcher's Fork]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/weapons/rapier.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Rapier" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Rapier]] || 1 || 1 || 0 || 2 gp
|}

=====Armor & Shields=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/chest/breastplate-scale-leather.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Leather Lamellar" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Leather Lamellar]] || 1 || 1 || 0 || 3 gp
|}

=====Consumables=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/lights/candle-unlit-grey.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Candle" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Candle]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 cp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/documents/document-bound-white-tan.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Cantrip Deck (Full Pack)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Cantrip Deck (Full Pack)]] || 1 || 0 || 1 || 20 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/oils/oil-of-revelation.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Formulated Sunlight" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Formulated Sunlight]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 40 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/misc/ladder-improvised.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Marvelous Miniature (Ladder)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Marvelous Miniature (Ladder)]] || 1 || 0 || 1 || 3 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/default-icons/consumable.svg" width="24" height="24" alt="Oil of Ownership (Lesser)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Oil of Ownership (Lesser)]] || 1 || 0.1 || 3 || 8 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/consumables/potions/potion-of-shared-memories.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Potion of Shared Memories" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Potion of Shared Memories]] || 1 || 0.1 || 1 || 4 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/alchemical-items/drugs/refined-pesh.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Refined Pesh" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Refined Pesh]] || 2 || 0.1 || 1 || 2 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/default-icons/consumable.svg" width="24" height="24" alt="peryton antler" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[peryton antler]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || —
|}

=====Equipment=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/brass-ear.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Brass Ear" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Brass Ear]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/crowbar.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Crowbar" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Crowbar]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 5 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/grapling-hook.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Grappling Hook" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Grappling Hook]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 1 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/lights/lantern-bullseye-signal-copper.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Lantern (Bull's Eye)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Lantern (Bull's Eye)]] || 1 || 1 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/held-items/marbles.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Marbles" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Marbles]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 2 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/playing-cards.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Marked Playing Cards" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Marked Playing Cards]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 1 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/equipment/neck/handkerchief-bandana-blue.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Masquerade Scarf" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Masquerade Scarf]] || 1 || 0 || 2 || 30 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/adventuring-gear/playing-cards.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Playing Cards" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Playing Cards]] || 1 || 0 || 0 || 5 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/weapon-property-runes/weapon-property-runes.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Returning" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Returning]] || 1 || 0 || 3 || 55 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/icons/sundries/survival/rope-wrapped-brown.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Rope" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Rope]] || 1 || 0.1 || 0 || 5 sp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/fundamental-weapon-runes/striking.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Striking" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Striking]] || 1 || 0 || 4 || 65 gp
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/runes/fundamental-weapon-runes/weapon-potency.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Weapon Potency (+1)" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Weapon Potency (+1)]] || 1 || 0 || 2 || 35 gp
|}

=====Treasure & Currency=====
{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! !! Item !! Qty !! Bulk !! Level !! Price
|-
| <html><img src="https://foundry.atkennedy.com/systems/pf2e/icons/equipment/treasure/currency/gold-pieces.webp" width="24" height="24" alt="Gold Pieces" style="vertical-align:middle;" /></html> || [[Gold Pieces]] || 10 || 1 || 0 || 1 gp
|}

</div>
</div>

''Last synced: 2026-06-20 01:33''
[[Category:Characters]]
